revision.text:
[[ja:大坊古墳 (福山市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Daibo Kofun | image = File:Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), gaikan.jpg | alternate_name = | part_of = | location = {{ill|Fukuyama|qid=Q661038|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.57519444444444|N|133.36786111111113|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted = JP-34 | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (or {{ill|Square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) | area = Diameter: 14m
Height: 5m | burial = Two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | SubsidiaryMounds = | built = Early 7th century | Deceased = | Mausoleum = | Historic Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site "Daibo Kofun" | TangibleHistoric Site = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = Round tumulus | map_dot_label = Daibo Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Daibo Kofun''' (大坊古墳) is an ancient Kofun (''kofun'') located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, Japan. Its type is generally considered to be a round tumulus, though some suggest it may be square. It has been Historic Site1 a prefectural historic site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The mound is situated on the eastern slope of a hill at the entrance of the Nakajo Valley, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture. The site has not been excavated. The tumulus is built on a flat surface cut into the hillside. Its type is slightly elongated north-south, with a diameter of approximately 14 meters and a height of about 5 meters.On-site intypeational plaque. A surrounding moat is visible behind the mound. The burial facility is a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southeast. This large chamber has a total length of 11.3 meters and uses finely polished granite stones. Although no grave goods have been confirmed, the mound's construction is estimated to date to the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}'s final stage in the early 7th century. Some sources extend this period to the first half of the 7th century.Koto Takashi, "Daibo Kofun," in {{Cite book|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|qq=}} The site was Historic Site1 as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in 1983. == burial == [[File:Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view|qq=}}]] [[File:Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber expanded view|qq=}}]] The burial facility consists of a two-tiered {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows: * Total length: 11.2 meters (or 11.3 meters) * Burial chamber (''gen-shitsu''): Length 5.32 meters, rear wall width 1.92 meters, height 1.91 meters * Passageway (''sen-dō''): Length 4.4–4.82 meters, entrance width 1.92 meters, height 1.92 meters The chamber utilizes large granite stones with polished surfaces, demonstrating a transitional style preceding the emergence of fully cut stone chambers. A stone pillar and a horizontal beam divide the burial chamber from the passageway.[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64264.html Daibo Kofun] (Fukuyama City Official Website). The back wall features a single upright stone, while the side walls of both the burial chamber and passageway are constructed with two large stones on each side. Two horizontally laid stones divide the burial chamber floor. The symmetrical arrangement of stones and the equal area of the burial chamber and passageway are distinguishing features. Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards rear wall) Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards passageway) Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards entrance) Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards burial chamber) Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama), entrance-2.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Daibo Kofun - Designated on November 7, 1983.[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120970.html Daibo Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education "Hotline Education Hiroshima"). == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * On-site intypeational plaque (Fukuyama City Board of Education, installed 2013) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Koto Takashi|year=1989|chapter=Daibo Kofun|title=[[Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia]]|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia|1989|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama)|qq=}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120970.html Daibo Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefectural Board of Education "Hotline Education Hiroshima" * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64264.html Daibo Kofun] - Fukuyama City Official Website {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|version=104540152|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大坊古墳 (福山市)]] {{Commons category|Daibo Kofun (Fukuyama)}} {{wikidata link|Q104007891}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:広島県の古墳]] [[Category:福山市の歴史]]
